name: AP Laboratories
principle: Laboratories are proof. They show whether an idea can survive implementation.

laboratories.title: KGB Studio
laboratories.question: Can a browser-based studio reduce the friction between idea, asset, scene, runtime, and playable game?
laboratories.thesis: Creative systems improve when the workflow makes relationships visible and turns repeated decisions into reusable patterns.
laboratories.application: Game tooling, asset systems, scene building, runtime packaging, and reusable production workflows.
laboratories.evidence: A growing modular studio built from real production needs rather than abstract feature lists.

laboratories.title: KGB Arcade
laboratories.question: Can games teach systems, feedback, constraints, and adaptation without becoming lectures?
laboratories.thesis: Play is a systems-thinking environment. Every good game teaches the player to read signals, adapt to feedback, and improve decisions under constraint.
laboratories.application: Browser games, arcade systems, toddler learning games, MiniMission control surfaces, and systems-based play.
laboratories.evidence: A live browser arcade with handcrafted games, mobile-first constraints, and a growing reusable engine approach.

laboratories.title: Hidden Systems Visual Language
laboratories.question: Can interfaces help people see relationships, dependencies, telemetry, and uncertainty before those forces become obvious failures?
laboratories.thesis: Visual language is not decoration. It is a cognitive instrument for revealing structure, pressure, state, and relationship.
laboratories.application: AP site design, Atlas surfaces, diagrams, MiniMission concepts, presentations, and framework maps.
laboratories.evidence: A shared design direction built around topology, nodes, overlays, signal panels, relationship lines, and tactical annotations.

laboratories.title: Zero Trust Reference Work
laboratories.question: Can Zero Trust be explained as outcomes, evidence, decisions, and enforcement instead of products, slogans, and activity lists?
laboratories.thesis: Security architecture improves when people can distinguish capability from compliance motion and outcomes from activities.
laboratories.application: Zero Trust frameworks, capability mapping, outcome models, executive explanation, and implementation guidance.
laboratories.evidence: Architecture-first models and explanatory frameworks derived from real enterprise implementation work.

laboratories.title: Code Shield
laboratories.question: Can defensive review workflows become clearer, faster, and more useful for builders?
laboratories.thesis: Security tools should reduce uncertainty and help builders understand what matters, not bury them under noise.
laboratories.application: AppSec review, secure development, code analysis, and practical security guidance.
laboratories.evidence: A practical project space for experimenting with lightweight defensive tooling and builder-friendly security feedback.

laboratories.title: Asymmetric Utility
laboratories.question: Can small local-first tools solve real workflow problems without requiring accounts, servers, subscriptions, or vendor lock-in?
laboratories.thesis: Useful tools should respect constraints. No backend is not a weakness when the problem can be solved locally and clearly.
laboratories.application: File utilities, metadata tools, zip tools, viewers, converters, and field-expedient browser utilities.
laboratories.evidence: A growing set of practical HTML/CSS/JS utilities designed around ownership, simplicity, portability, and low friction.
